hero.desc: Enterprise Architect and Technology Leader with 20+ years driving digital transformation in automotive, manufacturing, and financial services. TOGAF 9 and PMP certified. MIT Sloan and Stanford Engineering executive education.
about.label: About
about.cred.togaf: Enterprise Architecture and Project Management Professional
about.cred.patents: 12 U.S. + 1 German — connected vehicles, AI, cybersecurity
about.bio.1: I am a technology executive and enterprise architect with a track record of translating complex organizational strategy into scalable, high-impact technology platforms. My career spans Fortune 50 automotive OEMs, global manufacturing, and financial services — where I have consistently led architecture at the intersection of emerging technology and enterprise reality.
about.bio.2: At Ford Motor Company, I served as a standing member of the Autonomous Vehicle Technical Council and led enterprise architecture initiatives spanning connected vehicles, UBI frameworks deployed across 26 countries, and AV platform strategy. At DaimlerChrysler/FCA/Stellantis, I led post-merger application portfolio rationalization through structured architecture governance, and architected the Global Bill of Materials for Mopar Strategic Services.
about.bio.3: Across every engagement, my focus has been the same: build architecture that endures, enables business agility, and delivers measurable outcomes. I bring deep expertise in TOGAF-based EA governance, SOA/BIAN transformation, AI-driven integration platforms, and digital transformation at enterprise scale.
about.bio.4: What distinguishes my approach is the ability to operate at multiple altitudes — from enterprise strategy and governance to hands-on architecture design — and to translate that thinking across industries as different as automotive OEM, financial services, and industrial manufacturing.
about.bio.5: Throughout my career I have led and mentored cross-functional teams of architects and engineers, guiding both technical direction and individual professional growth across complex enterprise programs.
about.bio.6: I hold 13 granted patents (12 U.S., 1 German) spanning connected vehicles, artificial intelligence, and cybersecurity — and I publish regularly on technology strategy and the future of mobility.

tl.label: Thought Leadership
tl.intro: Strategic analysis on EV transformation, enterprise architecture, and the forces reshaping the automotive and technology industries.
tl.art1.title: When Detroit Bet $50B on EVs and Lost
tl.art1.excerpt: A strategic post-mortem on Detroit's EV investment write-downs — examining the architecture, strategy, and organizational decisions that led here.
tl.art2.title: The $50 Billion Skills Gap Nobody Talks About
tl.art2.excerpt: Why Detroit's EV write-downs reveal a deeper workforce and architecture challenge that financial metrics alone cannot capture.
tl.art3.title: Strategic Skills That Separate Automotive Leaders
tl.art3.excerpt: The leadership capabilities and strategic thinking that distinguish executives who navigate technology transformation from those who don't.
tl.art4.title: The Problem-Solving Toolkit Every Leadership Team Needs
tl.art4.excerpt: A practical framework for leadership teams navigating complex technology decisions, drawn from lessons in the automotive EV transformation.
tl.art5.title: The $30,000 Electric Vehicle Moment Is Here
tl.art5.excerpt: An analysis of the emerging affordable EV inflection point and what it means for automakers, suppliers, and the broader mobility ecosystem.
tl.badge.7: Bank of America Customer Advisory Panel
con.label: Consulting Services
con.intro: Fractional and interim advisory engagements for enterprises navigating digital transformation, platform modernization, and technology strategy.
con.s1.title: Enterprise Architecture Leadership
con.s1.desc: TOGAF-based EA governance, Architecture Review Board setup, capability roadmapping, and architecture rationalization for merger and acquisition scenarios. Engagements include ARB establishment, governance model design, and post-merger technology portfolio rationalization across automotive and financial services.
con.s2.title: Digital Transformation Strategy
con.s2.desc: End-to-end digital transformation advisory combining Westerman's Digital Mastery Framework with TOGAF ADM — from current-state assessment to target architecture. Delivered transformation roadmaps for Fortune 50 enterprises spanning connected vehicle platforms, dealer management systems, and banking technology modernization.
con.s3.title: Connected Vehicle & Mobility Platforms
con.s3.desc: Architecture strategy for connected vehicle ecosystems, OTA update frameworks, UBI platforms, and autonomous vehicle technical infrastructure. Hands-on experience architecting UBI frameworks deployed across 26 countries and serving on autonomous vehicle technical councils at the OEM level.
con.s4.title: AI & Integration Architecture
con.s4.desc: AI-driven integration platform design, SOA/BIAN transformation, API strategy, and assured-delivery messaging patterns for complex enterprise environments. Proven patterns include assured-delivery messaging, store-and-forward transaction recovery, and AI-augmented integration layer design for high-availability environments.
con.s5.title: Technology Due Diligence
con.s5.desc: Pre-acquisition and post-merger technology assessment — architecture health checks, technical debt quantification, and integration complexity analysis. Experience spanning post-merger scenarios at Tier 1 automotive OEMs and financial institutions, with structured assessment frameworks grounded in TOGAF ADM.
con.s6.title: IP Strategy & Patent Advisory
con.s6.desc: Emerging technology IP landscape analysis, innovation pipeline structuring, and patent strategy guidance for connected vehicles, AI, and cybersecurity domains. Holder of 13 granted patents with direct experience navigating the IP lifecycle from invention disclosure through grant.
